heading: Deposit of other moneys
label: 2043 Deposit of other moneys
text: Except for public moneys deposited under , each clerk of the United States courts shall deposit public moneys that the clerk collects into a checking account in the Treasury, subject to disbursement by the clerk. At the end of each accounting period, the earned part of public moneys accruing to the United States shall be deposited in the Treasury to the credit of the appropriate receipt accounts.section 2041 of this title
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised SectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)28:204331:725v(b)(related to clerks).(related to clerks), ; restated , .June 26, 1934, ch. 756, § 23(b)48 Stat. 1236Dec. 21, 1944, ch. 631, § 158 Stat. 845The words “Except for public moneys deposited under . . . public moneys” are substituted for “All fees and other collections other than moneys referred to in subsection (a) of this section” for consistency and because 31:725v(a) is superseded by 28:2041 and is not part of the revised title contained in section 1 of the bill. The word “Treasury” is substituted for “Treasurer of the United States” because of section 1 of Reorganization Plan No. 26 of 1950 (eff. , ), restated as section 321 of the revised title contained in section 1 of the bill. The text of 31:725v(b)(last sentence) is omitted as obsolete.section 2041 of this title64 Stat. 1280July 31, 1950
